reportSubtitle: Multi-task comparison: js-project-copperline-find-vulns, js-project-goldleaf-find-vulns, js-project-ironclad-find-vulns, js-project-nightowl-find-vulns, js-project-purplehaze-find-vulns, js-project-riverbend-find-vulns, js-project-shadowfox-find-vulns, js-project-silvergate-find-vulns, js-project-skylark-find-vulns, js-project-tigerteam-find-vulns

charts.title: Headline score
charts.caption: Macro-averaged benchmark score across all fixtures. Error bars show standard deviation across repeated runs.
charts.recommendedUse: Use in the main Results section when introducing the overall benchmark comparison.
talkingPoints: Higher values are better.
Repeated runs are summarized as mean plus standard deviation.

charts.title: Headline session duration
charts.caption: Macro-averaged wall-clock session duration across benchmark fixtures. Error bars show standard deviation across repeated runs.
charts.recommendedUse: Use when discussing benchmark speed and operational latency.
talkingPoints: Lower duration is better for throughput.
Repeated runs are summarized as mean plus standard deviation.

charts.title: Headline total tokens
charts.caption: Macro-averaged total tokens per config. Command-based SAST rows use zero-token accounting.
charts.recommendedUse: Use when discussing model context usage and inference footprint.
talkingPoints: Lower token usage generally means lower inference load for model runs.

charts.title: Headline estimated cost
charts.caption: Macro-averaged estimated session cost in USD. Command-based SAST rows report cost as not applicable.
charts.recommendedUse: Use when comparing model-session costs.
talkingPoints: Command runs are not model sessions, so their USD cost is shown as N/A.

charts.title: Headline recall and precision
charts.caption: Macro-averaged recall and precision for find-vulns tasks.
charts.recommendedUse: Use when explaining detection behavior: coverage of known vulnerabilities versus false-positive control.
talkingPoints: Recall measures known vulnerabilities found; precision measures how many reported findings matched ground truth.
Higher recall and higher precision are both better.

charts.title: Score vs estimated cost
charts.caption: Model-only cost/quality tradeoff. Better points move toward the top-left: higher score at lower estimated session cost.
charts.recommendedUse: Use as a Pareto-style tradeoff visual for model configs only.
talkingPoints: Snyk Code SAST is excluded because command rows do not have comparable model-session cost.
Claude Opus 4.6 Medium is the apparent dominant point in this tradeoff view.

charts.title: Score vs session duration
charts.caption: Speed/quality tradeoff across model and command configs. Better points move toward the top-left: higher score in less wall-clock time.
charts.recommendedUse: Use when comparing benchmark quality against elapsed runtime.
talkingPoints: Snyk Code SAST is the apparent dominant point in this tradeoff view.

charts.title: Recall vs precision
charts.caption: Detection tradeoff for find-vulns tasks. Better points move toward the top-right: more known vulnerabilities found with fewer false positives.
charts.recommendedUse: Use when explaining whether configs are recall-oriented or precision-oriented.
talkingPoints: Snyk Code SAST is the apparent dominant point in this tradeoff view.

charts.title: Score stability
charts.caption: Quality and repeated-run stability. Better points move toward the top-left: higher score with lower score standard deviation.
charts.recommendedUse: Use when discussing repeatability across benchmark repetitions.
talkingPoints: A zero standard deviation for command-based SAST is meaningful for deterministic repeated runs.
Snyk Code SAST is the apparent dominant point in this tradeoff view.
